titleStmt.title: Translation of NLS Adv. MS 15.1.18, no. 67

div.p: To all sons of Holy Mother Church, clerk and lay, present and future, Malcolm, son of Duncan earl of Fife, sends Greeting: Let those to come as well as those present know that I have granted and by this my charter made firm, to God and to Saint Andrew, and to the prior and religious community serving God at St Andrews now and in the future, the donations that Duncan the Earl, my father, made to them, that is, the church of Cupar, the church of Markinch, the church of Scoonie, the chapel of Kettle, with everything belonging to them. Wherefore I will that the aforesaid prior and community of the priory should hold and possess the churches of me and my heirs, as freely and quietly as the charters of my father bear witness. As witnesses: Merleswain; Adam of Ceres; Adam the Earl’s brother; Alan son of Simon; Odo the steward; William de Lascelles; Walter of Lundin; Hugh the butler; Albin the chaplain; Milo de Raiville; Geoffrey de Melville; and many others.
